Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Hi Alex / Priya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap: Novacorp is prioritizing a live technical deep dive to validate inference throughput for your mixed-traffic (short chat + long-form summarization) use cases, and you asked for a schedule that rotates across US/EU/APAC-friendly times so product teams in each region can join periodically.

Rather than a single fixed time, we often run a 3-band "Commuter Bands" approach that alternates who gets the most convenient slot on a weekly basis. It looks like this (example):

- Band A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 PT / 13:00 ET / Wed 02:00 SGT
- Band B (EU-friendly): Wed 15:00 CET / 09:00 ET / Thu 00:00 SG...

Cc: nina.garcia@acme-connectors.com
Date: 2026-04-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Integration: OAuth token failures in SDK v1.3 — urgent

Hi Camila,

We're seeing repeated 401s from Redwood's hosted endpoints when our customers use the Acme Connector SDK v1.3...
text:
From: support@acme-connectors.com
To: camila.reyes@redwood.com
Cc: nina.garcia@acme-connectors.com
Date: 2026-04-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Integration: OAuth token failures in SDK v1.3 — urgent

Hi Camila,

We're seeing repeated 401s from Redwood's hosted endpoints when our customers use the Acme Connector SDK v1.3. The failures began around 08:50 PDT and affect 18% of calls to /v1/generate with short prompts. We attached a trimmed log (acme-sdk-logs-20260410.txt) and a sample request/response.

Symptoms: token exchange succeeds, subsequent requests fail mid-stream with "invalid_token" and no retry indicated. Rolling back to v1.2 removes the error. No config changes on our side.

This is blocking our production customers and we've had two outage alerts. We need a triage call and clarity on SLA response times — our contract references a 2-hour response for P1. Please advise next steps...

Ava / team,

We're seeing a sharp spike in 429s starting ~01:30 UTC this morning after we moved a large cohort of traffic to the new enterprise tier. Impact: API calls from our ingestion pipeline are being rate-limited, causing downstream processing delays and timeouts for clinical syncs.

Quick details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings
- Sample org/project IDs: org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14
- Approx. 20% of requests returned 429, many followed by retries that compounding failures

Can you confirm whether any quota/tiering change rolled out today and whether you can temporarily raise limits while we stabilize? We're prepared to open a maintenance win...

Quick update from procurement: the PO is being routed but it's likely to clear in ~10–12 business days due to an internal audit. The pilot team still needs the environment to finish integration tests — otherwise we risk losing momentum.

Is there any way Redwood can keep one active demo seat and a small token allowance for the core integration team for two weeks until the PO hits? We can share the signed PO as soon as approvals are done; attached is the PO draft procurement asked us to prepare.

Appreciate any help — we really want to keep the pilot progressing.

Thanks,
Dana

ClearMed Health

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>

Date: Thu, 12 Mar 2026 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: SaaSFlow: slab pricing simulation & commit phasing

Ethan —

Thanks again for the deep dive today. Per our conversation I attached a couple of slab simulations so you can see the unit economics across different commit shapes. The attached slab_simulation_v2.xlsx includes:
- Monthly token bands (0-500M, 500M-2B, 2B+)
- Per-token rate at each band and effective blended rates for 3 commit sizes
- A model for 6-month ramp (0% month 1 -> 100% month 6) and a 12-month steady commit

Key assumptions called out in the sheet: throughput smoothing, auto-burst cap (25%), and prefix cache savings (estimated 12%).

A few things to highlight up front:
- We can offer a slab discount starting at 15% off hosted baseline for a ~1B token/mo commit.
- For multi-month phasing we common...
